metadata.description: 100 pre-labelled attack logs across 10 attack scenarios for simulate endpoint and DB seeding

logs.log_text: Failed login attempt for user 'admin' from IP 192.168.1.20 - attempt 1 of 50
logs.pre_written_explanation: An initial failed login attempt was detected for the admin account from IP 192.168.1.20. A single failure may be a mistyped password but should be monitored.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Monitor this IP for further failed attempts. No immediate action needed at this stage.

logs.log_text: Failed login attempt for user 'admin' from IP 192.168.1.20 - attempt 10 of 50
logs.pre_written_explanation: Ten consecutive failed logins from the same IP targeting the admin account suggest an automated password guessing attack is underway.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Add IP 192.168.1.20 to a watchlist. Enable rate limiting on the login endpoint immediately.

logs.log_text: Failed login attempt for user 'admin' from IP 192.168.1.20 - attempt 25 of 50
logs.pre_written_explanation: Twenty-five consecutive failed login attempts from IP 192.168.1.20 strongly indicate an active brute-force attack against the admin account.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block IP 192.168.1.20 at the firewall. Notify the account owner and enforce CAPTCHA on the login page.

logs.log_text: SSH authentication failure: invalid password for root from 192.168.1.20 port 54321
logs.pre_written_explanation: An SSH brute-force is now targeting the root account. Successful compromise would grant full system control to the attacker.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block SSH from this IP. Disable root SSH login in sshd_config and switch to key-based authentication only.

logs.log_text: Account 'admin' locked after 30 failed login attempts from 192.168.1.20
logs.pre_written_explanation: The admin account lockout policy has triggered after 30 failures. The attacker may now pivot to other accounts.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Notify the admin user. Permanently block IP 192.168.1.20 and check whether other accounts are being targeted.

logs.log_text: Brute-force tool signature detected: Hydra/v9.4 user-agent from IP 192.168.1.20
logs.pre_written_explanation: The known brute-force tool Hydra has been fingerprinted, confirming this is a deliberate, automated attack rather than accidental login failures.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block all traffic from IP 192.168.1.20. Alert the incident response team and review all accounts for unauthorised access.

logs.log_text: Successful login for user 'backup_svc' from IP 192.168.1.20 after 47 failed attempts
logs.pre_written_explanation: CRITICAL: The attacker has successfully authenticated as backup_svc after 47 attempts. Authenticated access to the system is now confirmed.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Terminate the session immediately, reset backup_svc credentials, and revoke all active tokens. Isolate the system and begin investigation.

logs.log_text: Multiple accounts targeted from 192.168.1.20: admin, root, guest, backup_svc, dbadmin
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is conducting a credential stuffing attack across five system accounts simultaneously, indicating a targeted and sophisticated campaign.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Force password resets on all targeted accounts. Enable MFA for all administrative accounts immediately.

logs.log_text: Unusual login time: user 'backup_svc' active at 02:01 AM from new device 192.168.1.20
logs.pre_written_explanation: The compromised backup_svc account is now active at an unusual hour from the attacker's IP, confirming the account is under attacker control.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Disable backup_svc account immediately. Audit all actions performed by this account in the last 30 minutes.

logs.log_text: Firewall rule added by 'backup_svc' to allow inbound traffic on port 4444
logs.pre_written_explanation: The compromised account has opened port 4444, commonly used for reverse shells. The attacker is establishing a persistent backdoor.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Revert the firewall rule immediately. Isolate the server, revoke backup_svc credentials, and begin full forensic investigation.

logs.log_text: Port scan: IP 203.0.113.42 scanned 1024 ports on 10.0.0.5 in 4 seconds
logs.pre_written_explanation: An external IP performed a rapid scan of over 1000 ports, mapping available services on the internal server as the first step of an attack.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block IP 203.0.113.42 at the perimeter firewall and review which services are publicly exposed.

logs.log_text: SYN flood: 5000 SYN packets/sec from 203.0.113.42 targeting port 443
logs.pre_written_explanation: A SYN flood is overwhelming the HTTPS port with thousands of half-open connections per second, risking server resource exhaustion and downtime.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Enable SYN cookies on the server and block the source IP. Contact your ISP to filter upstream traffic if the flood persists.

logs.log_text: Nmap OS detection and service version probing from 203.0.113.42
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is using Nmap to identify the operating system and exact software versions, which are used to find known exploitable vulnerabilities.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block the scanning IP. Suppress OS fingerprinting responses in the firewall and ensure all software is patched.

logs.log_text: Open ports on 10.0.0.5: 22 (SSH), 80 (HTTP), 443 (HTTPS), 3306 (MySQL), 8080 (HTTP-Alt)
logs.pre_written_explanation: MySQL port 3306 is publicly accessible, which is a critical misconfiguration allowing potential direct database access from the internet.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Restrict MySQL port 3306 to internal network access only via firewall rules immediately.

logs.log_text: CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) probe from 203.0.113.42 on port 8080
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is probing for the critical Log4Shell vulnerability. If vulnerable Log4j is in use, remote code execution is possible with no authentication required.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Verify Log4j usage on port 8080 applications and apply patches or set LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS=true. Block the source IP.

logs.log_text: SQL injection probe from 203.0.113.42 on web application at port 80
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is probing the web application for SQL injection vulnerabilities that could allow full database access, modification, or deletion.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Enable WAF rules to block SQL injection patterns and audit all database queries to use parameterised inputs.

logs.log_text: Directory traversal attempt from 203.0.113.42: GET /../../../../etc/passwd HTTP/1.1
logs.pre_written_explanation: A directory traversal attack is attempting to read the system password file, which would reveal user account names for further targeted attacks.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block the IP and patch the web server to sanitise path inputs. Verify sensitive files are outside the web server document root.

logs.log_text: Admin panel enumeration from 203.0.113.42: probing /admin /wp-admin /phpmyadmin
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is scanning for exposed admin panels which could be targeted for authentication bypass or brute-force of administrator credentials.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Restrict admin paths to trusted IP ranges. Rename default admin URLs and enforce MFA on all management interfaces.

logs.log_text: Reverse shell: outbound connection from 10.0.0.5 to 203.0.113.42 on port 4444
logs.pre_written_explanation: CRITICAL: The server is connecting back to the attacker on port 4444. This confirms a successful reverse shell — the attacker now has remote code execution on the server.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Isolate server 10.0.0.5 from the network immediately. Block outbound traffic to 203.0.113.42 and begin forensic analysis.

logs.log_text: Data exfiltration: 2.3 GB transferred from 10.0.0.5 to 203.0.113.42 over 10 minutes
logs.pre_written_explanation: Over 2 gigabytes of data has been exfiltrated to the attacker's server. Sensitive files, databases, or credentials may have been stolen.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Cut network access to the server. Identify transferred files using netflow logs. Begin breach disclosure procedures if regulated data was involved.

logs.log_text: User 'www-data' executed sudo /bin/bash without prior authorisation on server-01
logs.pre_written_explanation: The web server process account attempted to spawn a root shell. This account should never require root access, indicating the web application has been exploited.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Remove sudo privileges from www-data. Investigate the web application for command injection vulnerabilities and audit new files on the server.

logs.log_text: SUID exploitation: /usr/bin/find executed with -exec /bin/sh by user 'appuser'
logs.pre_written_explanation: A known Linux privilege escalation technique is being used to abuse the SUID bit on the find binary to spawn a privileged shell.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Remove SUID bit from /usr/bin/find using chmod -s. Audit all SUID binaries using find / -perm -4000.

logs.log_text: Sudoers modified: appuser ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL added to /etc/sudoers
logs.pre_written_explanation: The sudoers file was modified to grant unrestricted passwordless root access to appuser. This is a persistence mechanism ensuring the attacker retains elevated privileges.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Revert the sudoers file immediately. Audit write access to /etc/sudoers and begin system isolation procedures.

logs.log_text: Backdoor user created: 'sysbackup99' added to /etc/passwd with UID 0
logs.pre_written_explanation: A hidden backdoor account with root-level UID 0 has been created to maintain persistent access even after the initial exploit is patched.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Remove the sysbackup99 account with userdel. Audit /etc/passwd for other unauthorised accounts and force password resets for all users.

logs.log_text: Kernel exploit: CVE-2022-0847 (Dirty Pipe) binary executed on server-01 by appuser
logs.pre_written_explanation: The Dirty Pipe kernel exploit was executed, allowing an unprivileged user to overwrite read-only files and gain root access, bypassing all standard permissions.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Update the Linux kernel to version 5.16.11 or later. Isolate the server and assume full compromise has occurred.

logs.log_text: Crontab modified by appuser: root cron job added to run reverse shell every 5 minutes
logs.pre_written_explanation: A malicious cron job has been added to the root crontab, establishing a persistent reverse shell that reconnects every 5 minutes even if terminated.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Remove the malicious cron entry. Audit all crontabs in /etc/cron.d and block outbound connections to the attacker IP at the firewall.

logs.log_text: Sensitive file read: /etc/shadow accessed by process running as 'appuser'
logs.pre_written_explanation: The shadow password file containing hashed credentials for all system users was read. The attacker can now attempt offline password cracking.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Force password resets for all system accounts. If passwords are reused across systems, update credentials on all related services immediately.

logs.log_text: Rootkit detected: kernel module 'sysmon_helper.ko' loaded from /tmp by root process
logs.pre_written_explanation: A rootkit kernel module has been loaded from /tmp, which can hide processes, files, and network connections from administrators, making the system extremely difficult to clean.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Do NOT attempt in-place cleanup. Take a full disk image for forensics, then reimage from a known-good backup.

logs.log_text: Audit logs cleared: /var/log/auth.log and /var/log/syslog truncated to zero bytes by root
logs.pre_written_explanation: Authentication and system logs have been wiped to destroy evidence, confirming the attacker has root access and is attempting to hide their activities.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Recover logs from your SIEM or remote syslog server. This confirms full system compromise. Begin incident response and preserve all remaining evidence.

logs.log_text: Data exfiltration: 4.7 GB archive /tmp/.hidden_data.tar.gz transferred via SCP to 10.0.0.10
logs.pre_written_explanation: Nearly 5 GB of data has been archived and exfiltrated. Combined with root access and log deletion, this represents a full system breach with confirmed data theft.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Isolate all affected systems. Notify management and legal. Begin breach disclosure assessment and engage external incident response if needed.

logs.log_text: Suspicious executable detected: invoice_2025.pdf.exe downloaded by user jsmith from 185.220.101.5
logs.pre_written_explanation: A double-extension executable disguised as a PDF invoice was downloaded. This is a classic phishing payload delivery technique used to trick users into running malware.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Quarantine the file immediately. Scan the endpoint with antivirus. Block the source IP and educate the user about double-extension phishing.

logs.log_text: Antivirus alert: Trojan.GenericKD.47293821 detected in C:\Users\jsmith\Downloads\invoice_2025.pdf.exe
logs.pre_written_explanation: Antivirus has confirmed the downloaded file is a known Trojan. The malware is designed to establish remote access and may harvest credentials or deploy additional payloads.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Isolate the endpoint from the network. Run a full system scan and verify the antivirus blocked execution. Review jsmith's recent email for the phishing source.

logs.log_text: PowerShell execution policy bypass: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\temp\update.ps1
logs.pre_written_explanation: A PowerShell script was executed with a policy bypass flag, which is a common technique used by malware to execute malicious scripts without triggering standard Windows security controls.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block PowerShell execution policy bypass via Group Policy. Investigate the contents of update.ps1 and determine if it was executed successfully.

logs.log_text: C2 beacon detected: outbound HTTPS connection from 10.0.0.55 to 185.220.101.5 every 60 seconds
logs.pre_written_explanation: The infected endpoint is sending regular beacon signals to a Command and Control server, confirming active malware communication and remote attacker control.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately isolate the endpoint. Block outbound connections to 185.220.101.5 and investigate the extent of C2 communication in network logs.

logs.log_text: Lateral movement: malware spreading to 3 network shares: \\fileserver01\share, \\fileserver02\data
logs.pre_written_explanation: The malware is actively spreading across the network by writing copies of itself to accessible file shares, indicating worm-like lateral movement capability.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Isolate all affected file servers. Disable network shares temporarily and scan all endpoints that accessed these shares in the last 24 hours.

logs.log_text: Shadow copy deletion: vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet executed on 10.0.0.55
logs.pre_written_explanation: All Windows Volume Shadow Copies (backups) have been deleted. This is the final preparation step before ransomware encryption, eliminating the ability to restore files from local backups.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: URGENT: Isolate all systems on this network segment immediately. Check whether offsite or cloud backups are still intact before the encryption begins.

logs.log_text: Mass file encryption started: 1200 files encrypted per minute on 10.0.0.55, extension .locked appended
logs.pre_written_explanation: Ransomware is actively encrypting files at high speed. Every second of delay results in more data being locked and rendered inaccessible.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: CRITICAL: Cut network and power to affected machines immediately if isolation cannot be performed cleanly. Do not pay the ransom until all recovery options are exhausted.

logs.log_text: Ransom note created: README_DECRYPT.txt written to Desktop, Downloads, and Documents on 10.0.0.55
logs.pre_written_explanation: Ransomware has completed encryption and dropped ransom notes in multiple folders. The attacker is demanding payment for the decryption key.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Preserve a copy of the ransom note for law enforcement. Do not contact the attackers. Assess clean backup availability and begin recovery procedures.

logs.log_text: Ransomware spreading to backup server: encryption activity detected on backupserver-01
logs.pre_written_explanation: The ransomware has propagated to the backup server, which now threatens the primary recovery mechanism. This is a deliberate targeting of backups to maximise ransom pressure.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately disconnect the backup server. Verify whether any offline or air-gapped backups exist. Contact cyber insurance provider and legal counsel.

logs.log_text: Data exfiltration before encryption: 8 GB uploaded from 10.0.0.55 to 185.220.101.5 via FTP
logs.pre_written_explanation: 8 GB of data was exfiltrated prior to encryption, confirming double extortion ransomware tactics where the attacker threatens to publish stolen data even if ransom is paid.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Assume all data on the affected system is in attacker hands. Begin breach notification procedures and engage a ransomware incident response specialist.

logs.log_text: User 'mwilson' accessed 340 customer records outside normal working hours at 11:48 PM
logs.pre_written_explanation: A user accessed a high volume of customer records late at night, outside their normal working pattern. This anomalous behaviour warrants investigation.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Flag mwilson's account for review. Check whether the access was business-justified and review DLP logs for any data being copied or emailed.

logs.log_text: Bulk download: mwilson exported 5000 customer records to CSV from CRM system
logs.pre_written_explanation: A bulk export of 5000 customer records to a portable CSV format was performed. This volume of data export is inconsistent with normal job responsibilities.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately alert the DLP team. Place a hold on the exported file if still on corporate systems. Notify HR and the manager for investigation.

logs.log_text: USB storage device connected to mwilson's workstation at 11:55 PM
logs.pre_written_explanation: A USB storage device was connected to the workstation shortly after the bulk data export, suggesting data may be being copied to a physical removable device.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block USB storage on this workstation via Group Policy. Retrieve the device if the user is on-site. Escalate to HR and security management.

logs.log_text: Email alert: mwilson forwarded 3 confidential documents to personal Gmail account mwilson88@gmail.com
logs.pre_written_explanation: Three confidential documents were forwarded to a personal email address, bypassing corporate data controls. This is a clear policy violation and potential data breach.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block outbound email to personal accounts via email DLP rules. Retrieve the emails from the sent folder and preserve as evidence. Notify legal and HR.

logs.log_text: Access attempt to restricted financial database by mwilson - access denied (no clearance)
logs.pre_written_explanation: The user attempted to access a financial database for which they have no authorisation. This targeted attempt at restricted data suggests premeditated data theft.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Review and tighten access controls on the financial database. Add mwilson's account to active monitoring and restrict access to current role only.

logs.log_text: mwilson searched internal wiki for: VPN bypass, encryption tools, anonymous file sharing
logs.pre_written_explanation: Search queries for anonymisation and encryption tools on the internal knowledge base suggest the user is researching methods to exfiltrate data without detection.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Flag these search terms in the SIEM. Consider placing the user on administrative leave pending HR and legal review. Preserve all logs immediately.

logs.log_text: Large print job by mwilson: 287 pages printed from customer database report at midnight
logs.pre_written_explanation: A large print job of customer database content at midnight provides another data exfiltration channel, bypassing digital DLP controls through physical documents.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Retrieve printed documents if the user is still on-site. Disable printing of sensitive data categories via print DLP. Document the print job as evidence.

logs.log_text: mwilson's badge access revoked but account still active - login from home IP 88.99.100.55 at 1:20 AM
logs.pre_written_explanation: A terminated employee whose physical badge was revoked is still remotely accessing the system, indicating a failure in the offboarding process.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately disable all active sessions and accounts for mwilson. Review and fix the offboarding checklist to ensure digital access is revoked alongside physical access.

logs.log_text: mwilson accessed competitor client contact list - 1200 records viewed in 8 minutes
logs.pre_written_explanation: The former employee accessed a competitor client contact list at high speed, consistent with automated scraping or bulk recording of contacts for competitive intelligence.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Preserve full audit logs of access. Notify legal counsel for potential breach of employment contract. Submit evidence to law enforcement if criminal activity is suspected.

logs.log_text: Account mwilson disabled by IT after security alert, but shared credentials found active on servicedesk01
logs.pre_written_explanation: Despite account termination, shared service desk credentials linked to the former employee were still active, providing continued access. Shared credentials are a major security control failure.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Rotate all shared credentials immediately. Audit all service accounts for shared passwords. Enforce individual accounts for all access with no exceptions.

logs.log_text: Email gateway alert: suspicious email received by 42 users, sender: it-support@comp4ny.com (spoofed)
logs.pre_written_explanation: A phishing email impersonating the IT support team was sent to 42 employees. The spoofed domain comp4ny.com closely mimics the real domain to deceive recipients.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Quarantine all instances of the email. Send an organisation-wide alert warning employees. Block the spoofed domain in the email gateway.

logs.log_text: User kpatel clicked phishing link in email: http://comp4ny-portal.xyz/login at 09:03 AM
logs.pre_written_explanation: An employee clicked the phishing link, visiting a credential harvesting page designed to steal their login credentials by mimicking the corporate portal.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block the phishing domain. Reset kpatel credentials immediately. Check whether credentials were submitted on the fake site using web proxy logs.

logs.log_text: Credential submission detected: kpatel submitted username/password on phishing site comp4ny-portal.xyz
logs.pre_written_explanation: Credentials were entered on the phishing site, meaning the attacker now has kpatel username and password in plaintext. Account takeover is imminent.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Reset kpatel password immediately and enforce MFA. Check for any logins using these credentials from unusual locations in the last 30 minutes.

logs.log_text: OAuth token theft: kpatel Microsoft 365 session token hijacked, used from IP 91.210.45.33 (Romania)
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker used the stolen credentials to hijack kpatel Microsoft 365 session token, bypassing MFA and gaining access to email, files, and Teams messages from Romania.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Revoke all active Microsoft 365 tokens for kpatel via Azure AD. Block sign-ins from the Romanian IP range and review Conditional Access policies.

logs.log_text: Email rule created: all inbound emails auto-forwarded to attacker@protonmail.com from kpatel mailbox
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker configured an auto-forward rule to silently copy all incoming emails to an external address, enabling ongoing intelligence gathering on the organisation.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Remove the forwarding rule immediately via Exchange admin. Audit all mailboxes for similar rules. Restrict the ability to create auto-forward rules via Exchange policy.

logs.log_text: Internal phishing: attacker using kpatel account to send phishing emails to 150 internal employees
logs.pre_written_explanation: The compromised account is now being weaponised to send phishing emails to internal staff. Emails from a trusted colleague are far more likely to succeed than external phishing.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Suspend kpatel account immediately. Recall all emails sent from this account in the last hour. Alert all recipients and scan for further compromised accounts.

logs.log_text: SharePoint access: attacker downloaded 3 confidential strategy documents from kpatel SharePoint
logs.pre_written_explanation: Three confidential business strategy documents were downloaded from the compromised user SharePoint account, resulting in sensitive corporate data leaving the organisation.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Audit all documents accessed via the compromised account. Apply Information Rights Management to prevent further downloads and revoke document access links.

logs.log_text: Teams meeting invite sent by kpatel account: fake IT support session requesting remote access
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is using the compromised account to invite employees to a fake IT support call, likely to social-engineer them into installing remote access tools.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Cancel the meeting invite. Alert all invitees via a separate channel. Remind staff that IT will never request remote access via unsolicited Teams calls.

logs.log_text: MFA fatigue attack: kpatel received 27 MFA push notifications in 10 minutes from IP 91.210.45.33
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is bombarding the user with MFA approval requests hoping fatigue will cause them to accidentally approve one. This technique has successfully bypassed MFA in many real attacks.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Switch MFA to number-matching to prevent accidental approvals. Contact kpatel directly via phone to warn them. Consider temporarily blocking sign-ins for this account.

logs.log_text: Business Email Compromise: attacker using kpatel account requested $47,000 wire transfer to CFO
logs.pre_written_explanation: A Business Email Compromise (BEC) attack is in progress, with the attacker using the trusted email account to fraudulently request a large financial transfer from the CFO.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: URGENT: Contact the CFO directly via phone to stop the transfer. Suspend the kpatel account. Report to financial institution if a transfer was initiated. Notify law enforcement.

logs.log_text: WAF alert: SQL injection pattern detected in login form parameter from IP 45.33.32.156
logs.pre_written_explanation: A Web Application Firewall detected SQL injection syntax in a login form input field. The attacker is testing the application for database vulnerabilities.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block IP 45.33.32.156. Review WAF rules to ensure SQL injection payloads are properly filtered and verify the application uses parameterised queries.

logs.log_text: SQL error logged: syntax error near ' OR 1=1-- in query from 45.33.32.156
logs.pre_written_explanation: A classic SQL injection bypass payload was submitted to the login form. The database error response confirms the application is vulnerable to SQL injection.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Fix the query immediately using parameterised statements or ORM. Disable verbose database error messages in production to prevent information disclosure.

logs.log_text: Authentication bypass: successful login as 'administrator' via SQL injection from 45.33.32.156
logs.pre_written_explanation: The SQL injection attack bypassed authentication entirely, granting the attacker administrator access to the application without knowing any credentials.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately invalidate all active sessions. Disable the login endpoint. Patch the SQL injection vulnerability and conduct a full review of all database queries.

logs.log_text: UNION SELECT attack: attacker extracting database schema via UNION-based injection from 45.33.32.156
logs.pre_written_explanation: A UNION-based SQL injection is being used to extract database schema information including table names, column names, and data types, mapping the full database structure.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block the attacker IP via WAF. Restrict database user permissions to only what is necessary. Patch all injection points and conduct a full code audit.

logs.log_text: Database dump: attacker extracted users table with 15,000 rows via SQL injection from 45.33.32.156
logs.pre_written_explanation: The entire users table containing 15,000 records has been extracted. This data likely includes email addresses, usernames, and possibly hashed or plain-text passwords.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Force password resets for all 15,000 affected accounts. Begin breach notification under applicable data protection regulations. Patch all injection vulnerabilities.

logs.log_text: Credit card data accessed: payments table extracted via SQL injection - 3,200 card records
logs.pre_written_explanation: Payment card records have been extracted via SQL injection, constituting a PCI-DSS breach. This triggers mandatory reporting obligations to card networks and affected customers.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately notify your payment processor and card brands (Visa, Mastercard). Engage a PCI forensic investigator. Mandatory breach notification is required.

logs.log_text: Blind SQL injection detected: boolean-based time-delay attacks from 45.33.32.156 on /api/products
logs.pre_written_explanation: A blind SQL injection is using deliberate time delays to extract data one bit at a time from an API endpoint, indicating the attacker is systematically mapping data even without direct output.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Apply input validation and parameterised queries to the /api/products endpoint. Implement query timeouts and monitor for unusually slow database queries.

logs.log_text: OS command execution via SQL: xp_cmdshell enabled and executed by attacker on MSSQL server
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker escalated from database access to operating system command execution using xp_cmdshell, a dangerous MSSQL stored procedure. The database server is now fully compromised.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Disable xp_cmdshell immediately. Isolate the database server. Audit all commands run via xp_cmdshell in the SQL Server error log and treat the OS as compromised.

logs.log_text: Stored XSS injected via SQL: malicious JavaScript inserted into product descriptions table
logs.pre_written_explanation: Malicious JavaScript was injected into the database via SQL injection. Every user who views the affected product pages will have the script execute in their browser, enabling session hijacking.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Remove all malicious entries from the products table. Implement Content Security Policy headers and apply HTML encoding to all database-driven output.

logs.log_text: Database server added to botnet: outbound IRC traffic to 45.33.32.156 from database server
logs.pre_written_explanation: The compromised database server is now connecting to a botnet command and control server via IRC, confirming full server compromise and enlistment in criminal infrastructure.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Isolate the database server immediately. Block outbound IRC traffic at the firewall. Reimage the server from a clean backup and audit all data for integrity.

logs.log_text: Traffic spike: inbound traffic increased from 200 Mbps to 9.4 Gbps in 30 seconds
logs.pre_written_explanation: Network traffic surged from normal levels to nearly 10 Gbps in 30 seconds, indicating the start of a volumetric DDoS attack designed to saturate the network pipe.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Activate DDoS mitigation appliance. Contact ISP to enable upstream traffic scrubbing. Identify and begin blackholing the most active attacking source IP ranges.

logs.log_text: UDP flood: 1.2 million UDP packets/sec from 847 unique IPs targeting port 53 (DNS)
logs.pre_written_explanation: A UDP flood attack targeting the DNS server is underway from a botnet of 847 IPs. DNS disruption would prevent users from resolving the organisation domain, effectively taking services offline.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Rate-limit UDP on port 53. Implement Response Rate Limiting (RRL) on DNS. Consider switching to a cloud-based DDoS-protected DNS provider.

logs.log_text: HTTP flood: 780,000 requests/sec to https://company.com/login from bot network
logs.pre_written_explanation: An HTTP flood is targeting the login endpoint with 780,000 requests per second, which will exhaust web server threads and database connection pools, causing complete service unavailability.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Enable CAPTCHA on the login page. Deploy a CDN with DDoS protection. Rate-limit requests per IP and implement connection limits at the load balancer.

logs.log_text: Web server down: company.com returning 503 Service Unavailable, response time >30 seconds
logs.pre_written_explanation: The web server is now completely overwhelmed. All users are experiencing service outage. The DDoS attack has achieved its objective of making the service unavailable.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Activate failover infrastructure if available. Route traffic through Cloudflare or AWS Shield. Communicate service status to customers via social media or status page.

logs.log_text: DNS amplification: attacker using open resolvers to amplify attack 40x, 380 Gbps traffic
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attack is using DNS amplification to multiply the attack volume 40 times, achieving 380 Gbps of traffic. This volume can overwhelm even well-provisioned networks and their upstream providers.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Engage ISP for null-route/blackhole routing. Contact your upstream transit providers. Provision additional bandwidth and activate any contracted DDoS protection services.

logs.log_text: Database connection pool exhausted: all 500 DB connections occupied by flood requests
logs.pre_written_explanation: All database connections are now occupied by the flood of requests, meaning legitimate users cannot perform any database operations. Data-dependent services are completely offline.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Implement connection queuing and limit connections per IP at the application layer. Scale database connection pools via read replicas if available.

logs.log_text: SSL renegotiation attack: attacker forcing expensive SSL handshakes, CPU at 100% on load balancer
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is exploiting the computational cost of SSL/TLS handshakes to exhaust CPU resources on the load balancer. This layer-7 attack bypasses many volumetric defences.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Disable SSL renegotiation on the load balancer. Add additional load balancer capacity. Implement TLS session resumption to reduce handshake CPU cost.

logs.log_text: Slowloris attack detected: 12,000 partial HTTP connections kept open from 345 IPs
logs.pre_written_explanation: A Slowloris attack is keeping thousands of HTTP connections open indefinitely by sending partial headers, exhausting the web server connection limit without generating high traffic volume.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Set aggressive connection timeouts on the web server. Limit the maximum number of connections per IP. Implement mod_reqtimeout on Apache or equivalent on Nginx.

logs.log_text: Payment gateway timeout: all payment transactions failing due to DDoS impact on API gateway
logs.pre_written_explanation: The DDoS attack has cascaded to affect the payment processing API, causing all customer transactions to fail. Every minute of downtime results in direct financial loss to the business.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Activate backup payment gateway provider. Notify customers of payment issues via status page. Quantify financial impact for insurance claim and management reporting.

logs.log_text: Attack subsiding: traffic returning to 1.2 Gbps. Total downtime: 47 minutes. Estimated revenue loss: $94,000
logs.pre_written_explanation: The DDoS attack has ended after 47 minutes of downtime resulting in significant revenue and reputational impact. Post-incident analysis is now critical to prevent recurrence.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Conduct post-incident review. Invest in permanent DDoS mitigation service. Document the attack timeline, financial impact, and technical response for management and insurance.

logs.log_text: Unusual outbound traffic: 10.0.0.88 transferring 500 MB to unknown external IP 91.108.4.77
logs.pre_written_explanation: An internal server is transferring an unusually large amount of data to an unknown external IP address, which is inconsistent with normal business traffic patterns.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block outbound traffic to 91.108.4.77. Investigate what process on 10.0.0.88 is initiating the connection and identify what data is being transferred.

logs.log_text: DNS tunneling detected: 10.0.0.88 making 4000 DNS queries/min to subdomain c2.evilsite.xyz
logs.pre_written_explanation: Data is being covertly exfiltrated by encoding it inside DNS query subdomains, a technique that bypasses many firewall rules since DNS traffic is rarely blocked.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block DNS queries to evilsite.xyz. Implement DNS monitoring to detect anomalous query volumes and restrict DNS to internal resolvers only.

logs.log_text: Cloud storage upload: 2.1 GB uploaded to Dropbox from server 10.0.0.88 outside business hours
logs.pre_written_explanation: A large upload to Dropbox occurred outside business hours from a server account, suggesting automated data theft to a cloud storage location that may be controlled by an attacker.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block cloud storage services from server accounts. Audit Dropbox account linked to this system. Use CASB solution to monitor and control cloud upload activity.

logs.log_text: HTTPS exfiltration: data encoded in HTTPS POST body to 91.108.4.77, 180 requests in 5 minutes
logs.pre_written_explanation: Encrypted HTTPS POST requests are being used to exfiltrate data, making content inspection difficult. The high frequency of requests confirms automated data theft is in progress.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Implement SSL inspection on the egress firewall to inspect encrypted traffic. Block outbound HTTPS to uncategorised or newly registered domains.

logs.log_text: Database backup file copied: prod_database_backup_2025.sql.gz accessed by unauthorised process
logs.pre_written_explanation: A full production database backup file was accessed by an unauthorised process. If exfiltrated, this single file would give the attacker the entire database contents in one action.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Restrict backup file access to specific service accounts only. Encrypt backup files at rest. Audit the process that accessed the file and determine if exfiltration occurred.

logs.log_text: Employee PII accessed: HR database table employees_personal_data queried for all 8,400 records
logs.pre_written_explanation: The entire employees personal information table containing 8,400 records was queried by an unauthorised process, potentially including names, addresses, national IDs, and bank details.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately notify HR leadership. Begin data protection breach assessment under GDPR or applicable law. Forensically investigate how the database was accessed.

logs.log_text: Source code repository cloned: entire Git repository company-core-product cloned by unknown IP
logs.pre_written_explanation: The entire source code repository for the core product was cloned. This gives attackers full access to proprietary algorithms, hardcoded credentials, and security control implementations.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Rotate all secrets and API keys found in the codebase immediately. Audit the repository for hardcoded credentials using tools like GitLeaks. Restrict repository access.

logs.log_text: Steganography detected: 140 JPEG images with hidden encoded data uploaded to public S3 bucket
logs.pre_written_explanation: Data is being covertly hidden inside image files using steganography and uploaded to a public cloud bucket, a sophisticated technique to exfiltrate data while evading DLP tools that only scan for text content.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block writes to public S3 buckets. Implement DLP tools with steganography detection capability. Analyse the uploaded images to determine what data was hidden inside them.

logs.log_text: Email exfiltration: 44 emails with attachments sent to external domain from no-reply@company.com
logs.pre_written_explanation: Automated emails with attachments were sent from a service account to an external domain, using the organisation email infrastructure to exfiltrate data while bypassing outbound network controls.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Block bulk outbound email from service accounts. Audit all email sent from no-reply in the last 24 hours. Apply email DLP rules to scan attachments for sensitive content.

logs.log_text: Total exfiltration summary: 14.7 GB data transferred out of network over 45 minutes via 5 channels
logs.pre_written_explanation: Nearly 15 GB of data was exfiltrated through five separate channels over 45 minutes. The use of multiple channels indicates a sophisticated, planned operation to maximise data theft while evading detection.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Initiate full incident response. Preserve all network logs. Identify all data categories exposed and begin mandatory breach notifications. Engage forensic investigation team.

logs.log_text: Unknown exploit: web server crashed with segmentation fault from malformed HTTP/2 header from 5.188.62.14
logs.pre_written_explanation: A specially crafted HTTP/2 request caused the web server to crash with a memory error, suggesting an unknown vulnerability is being probed or exploited for the first time.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Capture the full crash dump. Temporarily disable HTTP/2 and revert to HTTP/1.1. Notify the web server vendor immediately with the crash details.

logs.log_text: Heap overflow detected: abnormal memory allocation pattern in OpenSSL library on port 443 from 5.188.62.14
logs.pre_written_explanation: A heap overflow has been triggered in the OpenSSL library, which handles all encrypted traffic. Successful exploitation could allow arbitrary code execution with the privileges of the web server process.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately patch or update OpenSSL. If no patch is available, consider disabling TLS until a workaround is found. Capture memory dumps for vulnerability analysis.

logs.log_text: Novel shellcode detected: IDS signature mismatch - payload does not match any known exploit signatures
logs.pre_written_explanation: The intrusion detection system detected shellcode execution that matches no known exploit signatures, strongly suggesting an unpatched zero-day vulnerability is being weaponised.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Isolate the affected server. Capture all traffic from the attacking IP. Submit the payload to antivirus vendors and CISA for analysis. Do not attempt to clean in place.

logs.log_text: Remote code execution confirmed: calculator.exe spawned by web server worker process on WIN-SERVER-02
logs.pre_written_explanation: Remote code execution has been confirmed. The web server process spawned calculator.exe, a classic proof-of-concept used by attackers to demonstrate code execution before deploying real payloads.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Immediately isolate WIN-SERVER-02. The server is fully compromised. Begin forensic acquisition before remediation. Alert your security vendor and report to CISA if critical infrastructure is involved.

logs.log_text: Exploit kit deployed: attacker uploaded exploit framework tools to C:\Windows\Temp\ on WIN-SERVER-02
logs.pre_written_explanation: A full exploit toolkit has been uploaded to the compromised server, indicating the attacker intends to use this machine as a pivot point for lateral movement throughout the network.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Disconnect WIN-SERVER-02 from all networks. Prevent lateral movement by reviewing all trust relationships and shared credentials between this server and others.

logs.log_text: Lateral movement: attacker pivoting from WIN-SERVER-02 to 4 internal servers using Pass-the-Hash technique
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is using extracted password hashes to authenticate to additional servers without knowing the actual passwords, rapidly expanding their foothold across the internal network.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Force Kerberos ticket invalidation and NTLM hash rotation across the domain. Enable Credential Guard on all Windows servers. Isolate all 4 newly compromised servers.

logs.log_text: Domain controller targeted: attacker attempting DCSync attack to dump all AD credentials from DC-01
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker is attempting to replicate all Active Directory credentials using the DCSync technique. If successful, they will obtain password hashes for every user in the entire domain.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: CRITICAL: Block DCSync replication from non-DC machines immediately. If successful, all Active Directory credentials must be reset. Enable Protected Users security group for all privileged accounts.

logs.log_text: Golden ticket attack: Kerberos TGT forged using extracted KRBTGT hash, valid for 10 years
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker has created a Kerberos Golden Ticket using the extracted KRBTGT hash. This forged ticket grants unrestricted access to all domain resources for up to 10 years, even after password resets.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Reset the KRBTGT account password TWICE with a 10-hour gap between resets to invalidate all forged tickets. This is a full domain compromise event requiring complete rebuild assessment.

logs.log_text: Zero-day reported: vulnerability CVE-2025-99872 in OpenSSL 3.1.2 published 2 hours after this attack started
logs.pre_written_explanation: A CVE has now been published for the OpenSSL vulnerability exploited in this attack, confirming this was a zero-day. The organisation was attacked before any patch existed.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Apply the vendor patch immediately across all OpenSSL instances. Use this incident to advocate for faster patch cycles and virtual patching via WAF for future zero-days.

logs.log_text: Full domain compromise: attacker has admin access to all 47 domain-joined systems. Incident severity: P1
logs.pre_written_explanation: The attacker has achieved full domain administrator access across all 47 systems, representing total network compromise. This is the most severe possible outcome of the attack chain.
logs.pre_written_mitigation: Declare a P1 major incident. Engage your incident response retainer immediately. Consider a full domain rebuild. Notify regulators, legal counsel, and cyber insurance within required timeframes.

brute_force.name: Brute Force Attack
brute_force.description: Attacker repeatedly guesses passwords using Hydra tool until gaining access to backup_svc, then establishes a backdoor

timeline.description: Repeated failures targeting root and admin

port_scan.name: Port Scan to Intrusion
port_scan.description: Attacker scans network, discovers vulnerable services, exploits a web app, achieves reverse shell, and exfiltrates 2.3 GB

privilege_escalation.name: Insider Privilege Escalation
privilege_escalation.description: Low-privilege user escalates to root, installs rootkit, clears logs, and exfiltrates 4.7 GB

malware.name: Ransomware Attack
malware.description: Phishing email delivers ransomware that spreads via network shares, deletes backups, encrypts 1200 files/min, and exfiltrates 8 GB

timeline.description: Files encrypted, ransom note, backup targeted, data exfil

insider_threat.name: Malicious Insider
insider_threat.description: Disgruntled employee exfiltrates 5000 customer records via USB, email, and cloud storage before and after account termination

phishing.name: Phishing & Account Takeover
phishing.description: Spoofed email compromises employee credentials leading to M365 account takeover, BEC fraud attempt of $47,000, and internal phishing

timeline.description: Spoofed email sent, user clicks link
MFA fatigue attack, BEC wire transfer request

sql_injection.name: SQL Injection to Full DB Compromise
sql_injection.description: SQL injection bypasses authentication, extracts 15,000 user records and 3,200 payment cards, then achieves OS-level command execution

timeline.description: WAF detection and SQL error confirmation
Schema dump, users and payments extracted
OS command execution, stored XSS, botnet

ddos.name: DDoS Attack (47-min Outage)
ddos.description: Multi-vector DDoS combining UDP flood, HTTP flood, DNS amplification, and Slowloris causes 47-minute outage with $94,000 revenue loss

data_exfiltration.name: Multi-Channel Data Exfiltration
data_exfiltration.description: Sophisticated attacker uses DNS tunneling, steganography, and 3 other channels to exfiltrate 14.7 GB including HR data, source code, and payment cards

timeline.description: DB backup, HR records, source code

zero_day.name: Zero-Day Exploit to Full Domain Compromise
zero_day.description: Unknown OpenSSL vulnerability exploited to gain RCE, pivot to 47 servers via Pass-the-Hash, extract all AD credentials via Golden Ticket attack
